chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Application No.S- 645 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. 1.For orders on office objections 2.For hearing of Bail Application. Mr. Alam Sher Bozdar, Advocate along with applicant. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, Deputy P.G for the State. Date of Hearing & Order: 09-10-2023 O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J.- It is alleged in FIR that applicant had obtained a loan of Rs. 10,00,000/- from complainant in January, 2022 for construction of his house, which he undertook to pay him in eight months and issued him a cheque of above amount, which on presentation in the bank for encashment was dishonored. Hence, FIR. 2. Learned counsel for applicant has pleaded for conformation of interim pre-arrest bail on the grounds of one month delay in registration of FIR and lack of evidence. Learned Deputy P.G for the State has submitted that except a word of complainant and witnesses, no documentary proof is available on record. 3. I have perused material available on record. It appears that complainant did not produce any documentary evidence of extending loan of that amount to applicant or the manner he had arranged the said amount all of sudden and given to the complainant. More so, before registration of FIR, applicant had filed an application under Section 22-A CrPC alleging robbery of his cheque book by the complainant. Hence, the case of further enquiry is made out and false implication of applicant cannot be ruled out. 2 4. Accordingly, this bail application is allowed in the given facts and circumstances and the interim pre-arrest bail earlier granted to the applicant by this Court is confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 5.

Hence, the case of further enquiry is made out and false implication of applicant cannot be ruled out. 2 4. Accordingly, this bail application is allowed in the given facts and circumstances and the interim pre-arrest bail earlier granted to the applicant by this Court is confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 5. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial Court while deciding the case on merits. JUDGE Ahmad
